content.full_text:
CITY OF SAN LUIS OBISPO
OFFICE OF THE MAYOR AND CITY COUNCIL
Post Office Box 8100 • San Luis Obispo, CA 93403-8100 • 805/549-7111

April 29, 1991

Mr. John Wallace
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Wallace:

I am seeking written confirmation advising me of potential conflict of interest related to my position as a City Council member and as an employee with a private company. In brief, these are the facts:

o    I'm a member of the City Council, elected in November 1989.

o    I work part-time (10-20 hours/week) as a wage employee with Perspective Planning. I have no ownership nor partnership relationship with Perspective Planning. I am one of four employees.

o    Two weeks ago, the owner of Perspective Planning signed a contract with Terry Simons, owner of Development Services Incorporated (DSI), to provide planning and environmental services for a project that DSI is proposing to the County Board of Supervisors, for a parcel of land located several miles from the city limits of San Luis Obispo. The contract is worth approximately $1,000 to Perspective Planning.

o    I will be paid approximately $200 to work on the DSI contract.

o    Mr. Simons, owner of DSI, occasionally appears before the San Luis Obispo City Council for the review and approval of projects he proposes to develop within the city limits.

The question is: By indirectly working for Mr. Simons as a part-time employee of Perspective Planning, will I have a conflict of interest if Mr. Simons appears before the San Luis Obispo City Council in the future? Will I have to step down from reviewing projects that he proposes to the City Council?

To reiterate our telephone conversation today, Mr. Wallace, you advised that I probably would not have a conflict for the following reasons:


Fair Political Practices Commission
April 29, 1991

Page 2

o    I am not an owner nor partner of Perspective Planning.

o    The project that Perspective Planning is working on for DSI will not be
reviewed by me as a City Council member, since it is located in the
unincorporated County.

o    Should DSI propose a project to the San Luis Obispo City Council in the
future, no one at Perspective Planning will have any financial interest in that
project.

Thank you for advising me on this matter.

Sincerely,

Bill Roalman

BILL ROALMAN
City Council Member

BR:ss

sections.question: By indirectly working for Mr. Simons as a part-time employee of Perspective Planning, will I have a conflict of interest if Mr. Simons appears before the San Luis Obispo City Council in the future? Will I have to step down from reviewing projects that he proposes to the City Council?
sections.question_synthetic: Does a City Council member have a conflict of interest by working part-time for a planning firm that contracts with a developer who occasionally appears before the Council, and must the Council member recuse from reviewing that developer's future projects?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from the public, not an FPPC response/advice letter. The document references telephone advice given by FPPC staff (Mr. Wallace) but does not contain a formal written FPPC conclusion or advice letter. The document quality is poor with OCR artifacts affecting readability. No formal CONCLUSION section is present.

embedding.summary: A San Luis Obispo City Council member seeks written confirmation regarding potential conflicts of interest arising from his part-time employment with Perspective Planning, which has contracted to provide services for a developer (DSI owner Terry Simons) who occasionally appears before the City Council. The letter references prior telephone advice from FPPC staff indicating no conflict exists based on the circumstances presented.
